dependencies: Multiple suppliers for most key raw materials and energy sources
Single source suppliers for certain raw materials with contingency plans
Key raw materials include paraxylene, propane, propylene, cellulosic biopolymers, polyvinyl alcohol, methanol, fatty alcohol
Key energy sources include natural gas, coal, and electricity
risks: Temporary shortages of raw materials and energy may occasionally occur
Continuous availability and cost subject to unscheduled plant interruptions during high demand
War or other outbreak of hostilities or terrorism
Breakdown or degradation of transportation infrastructure

regulatory_risks: Complex health, safety, and environmental laws and regulations in U.S. and internationally requiring significant expenditures
Violations of environmental, health or safety laws could result in restrictions, prohibitions, damages, penalties, fines, civil or criminal sanctions
Future developments and more stringent environmental regulations may require significant expenditures
Changes in health, safety, environmental, chemical control regulations and testing requirements
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and similar foreign anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws
U.S. or foreign government regulations on trade sanctions and embargoes
Financial, regulatory, physical and transition risks associated with climate change

Future changes in legislation and regulation associated with physical impacts of climate change may increase likelihood of carbon requirements, regulation of greenhouse gas emissions, and energy policy
Changes to income tax laws and regulations or interpretation in jurisdictions where company operates
U.S. Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act
Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) Process Safety Management program requirements
Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act

sanction_risks: Recent and proposed changes in U.S. trade policy and resulting retaliatory actions by other countries, including China
U.S. and foreign countries may adopt or increase restrictions on foreign trade or investment, including currency exchange controls, tariffs or other taxes, or limitations on imports or exports
Ongoing Russia/Ukraine and Middle East conflicts
